name: Electrical Workers Local 58
description_short: Electrical Workers Local 58 Credit Union is a member-owned, NCUA-insured credit union serving the Detroit area with auto loans, personal loans, savings, and checking accounts.
description_long:
Electrical Workers Local 58 Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution headquartered in Detroit, Michigan at 1366 Porter Street. The credit union serves members with traditional consumer banking and lending products, with a focus on competitive rates and personalized service from experienced staff. The organization operates under NCUA (National Credit Union Administration) insurance, providing member deposits with federal protection.

The credit union offers a range of financial products including savings accounts, checking accounts, auto loans (new and used vehicles), personal loans, recreational vehicle loans, and a Visa credit card with no annual fee. They provide online banking through their IT.ME247 platform, a mobile application for account management, and recently completed a system upgrade as of November 1, 2025, which required all members to create new accounts. Members can apply online for loans and manage accounts through multiple channels.

Electrical Workers Local 58 distinguishes itself through competitive auto loan rates (4.99% for new cars, 5.49% for used cars), personal loan rates at 8.99%, and a Visa card at 10.90% APR with no annual fees. The credit union operates a single physical branch location with limited hours (Monday-Friday 9am-4pm, with Friday lunch closure 11:30am-12:30pm), emphasizing its local, community-focused approach. Members benefit from the credit union's philosophy that the institution belongs to its members and profits are returned to them through competitive rates and service quality.

The primary limitation is the single branch location in Detroit with restricted hours, making access challenging for members outside the immediate area or those needing evening/weekend services. The recent system upgrade requirement created a barrier to account access. While the credit union advertises competitive rates and knowledgeable staff, there is limited public information about membership eligibility criteria, account minimum balances, or fee structures beyond rates advertised.
services: New car auto loans (4.99% featured rate)
Used car auto loans (5.49% featured rate)
Visa credit card (10.90% APR, no annual fee)
pros: Competitive auto loan rates: 4.99% for new cars and 5.49% for used cars
Personal loans at 8.99% APR, below typical credit union averages
Visa credit card with 10.90% APR and no annual fee
NCUA-insured member deposits with federal protection up to $250,000
Mobile app and online banking (IT.ME247) for account management
Member-owned structure means profits are returned to members rather than shareholders
Experienced, knowledgeable staff described by members as helpful and understanding
cons: Single physical branch location (1366 Porter, Detroit, MI) limits accessibility for members outside Detroit area
Restricted hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm only (closed Saturday-Sunday and during lunch Friday 11:30am-12:30pm)
Recent system upgrade (November 2025) required all members to create new accounts, causing access disruption
Limited online information about membership eligibility, minimum balance requirements, and fee schedules
No mention of extended services like investment products, business accounts, or mortgage lending
best_for: Detroit-area residents and electrical workers seeking an auto loan with competitive rates
Members wanting a no-fee Visa card with reasonable rates from a local institution
Individuals who qualify for membership and can access the single Porter Street location during business hours
Savers looking for NCUA insurance protection and member-owned institutional benefits
diagnosis: Electrical Workers Local 58 is best for members living in or near Detroit who need competitive-rate auto or personal loans and want to bank with a member-owned institution. The primary caveat is the single-branch, limited-hours operation makes this impractical for anyone outside Detroit or those needing evening/weekend access.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Electrical Workers Local 58 — NCUA-insured credit union in DETROIT, MI. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
